---
title: "Ethics of Modern Piracy"
author: "Ryan McMurray"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    width: 1600
    height: 900
---

## Introduction

- Popular media romanticizes piracy  
- Modern piracy = digital (movies, books, software)

This project evaluates piracy through:

- Inequality  
- Media preservation  
- Censorship  

## Overcoming Inequality

- Educational resources are expensive  
  - Textbooks  
  - Academic journals  
- Paywalls limit access to knowledge  
    - Hurts individuals  
    - Helps creators financially  

## Preservation & Censorship

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Media Preservation

- Digital media can disappear  
- Old games, films, books become inaccessible  
- Piracy acts as:
    - A digital archive  
    - A cultural preservation tool  

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Circumventing Censorship

- Governments restrict information  
- Piracy enables access to:
  - News  
  - Education  
  - Media   

:::

::::
**Piracy supports freedom of information**

## Conclusion

- Piracy is not purely good or bad  
- It exists in an ethical gray area  

### Piracy

- Can reduce inequality  
- Can preserve culture  
- Can bypass unjust censorship  

**Ethical judgment depends on how and why piracy is used**
